title: ClientInstanceRecovery v1
description: Machine-readable schema for a pod-layer artifact that recovers client access after a detachment, device loss, migration, or other interruption, while reusing the same participant-over-channel boundary as the attachment and detachment artifacts.

recovery/id.description: Stable identifier of this client-instance recovery artifact.

recovered-at.description: Timestamp when the recovery path was accepted.

serving-node/id.description: Serving node that accepts the recovered client access.

client-instance/id.description: Concrete client, device, or install instance being recovered or re-established.

recovery/mode.description: Coarse recovery mode used for the resumed client path.

recovery/from-detachment-id.description: Reference to the detachment artifact or equivalent termination event being recovered from.

recovery/token-ref.description: Optional reference to a migration bundle, export token, or recovery token used in the process.

participant-bind.description: Participant-scoped bind proving who is resuming access over the already established channel.

proof/node-attestation.description: Optional future-facing serving-node attestation over the recovery artifact.

policy_annotations.description: Optional local or federation policy annotations that do not change the core recovery semantics.
